Title: Firebase Realtime Database
URL: https://firebase.google.com/docs/database
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
Preferred Video The Firebase Realtime Database is a cloud-hosted database. Data is stored as JSON
[...]
and synchronized in realtime to every connected client. When you build
cross-platform apps with our Apple platforms, Android, and JavaScript SDKs, all of your
clients share one Realtime Database instance and automatically receive updates with
the newest data.
[...]
requests, the Firebase Realtime Database
[...]
| Accessible from Client Devices | The Firebase Realtime Database can be accessed directly from a mobile device or web browser; there's no need for an application server. Security and data validation are available through the Firebase Realtime Database Security Rules, expression-based rules that are executed when data is read or written. |
[...]
| Scale across multiple databases | With Firebase Realtime Database on the Blaze pricing plan, you can support your app's data needs at scale by splitting your data across multiple database instances in the same Firebase project. Streamline authentication with Firebase Authentication on your project and authenticate users across your database instances. Control access to the data in each database with custom Firebase Realtime Database Security Rules for each database instance. |
[...]
The Realtime Database provides a flexible, expression-based rules language,
called Firebase Realtime Database Security Rules, to define how your data should be
structured and when data can be read from or written to. When integrated with
Firebase Authentication, developers can define who has access to what data, and how
they can access it.
[...]
- Firebase Hosting hosts the HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for your website as well as other developer-provided assets like graphics, fonts, and icons.

[...]
using Firebase Realtime Database Security Rules.

Title: What's new from Firebase at Cloud Next 2026
URL: https://firebase.blog/posts/2026/04/cloud-next-2026-announcements
Published: 2026-04-22T11:30:00.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
Firebase Data Connect lets you connect your mobile and web client apps to the power of Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL. Today, we’re evolving Firebase Data Connect into Firebase SQL Connect, a robust SQL solution with support for realtime syncing, offline cache, and native SQL—all available via a no-cost trial.
[...]
- Realtime sync lets you transform your relational data into live, in-app features, such as a newsfeed or a gaming leaderboard.
- Offline cache support makes your app feel more responsive even with low or no connectivity, ensuring users always have a smooth experience.
- Native SQL support means you can now write your queries and mutations in SQL instead of GraphQL and get direct access to all of the PostgreSQL extensions and tools.
[...]
Historically, enterprise customers faced toil when managing Firebase in tandem with broader Google Cloud infrastructure. The new Firebase integration with Application Design Center (ADC) addresses this challenge by offering a unified model for deploying and managing the resources used by your mobile and web client apps, such as Firestore, App Check, Authentication, and Firebase AI Logic, in the same way you manage the rest of your Google Cloud infrastructure.
[...]
Firestore and Firebase Authentication are now available in Google AI Studio so you can build full-stack apps using natural language. Using Cloud Run as its serverless runtime, Google AI Studio now supports server-side code, storing data with Firestore, drafting and deploying Firestore Security Rules, and providing user authentication and authorization through Firebase Authentication.

---

Title: Firebase Authentication
URL: https://firebase.google.com/docs/auth
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
Firebase Authentication provides backend services, easy-to-use SDKs, and ready-made UI
libraries to authenticate users to your app. It supports authentication using
passwords, phone numbers, popular federated identity providers like Google,
Facebook and Twitter, and more.
[...]
Firebase Authentication integrates tightly with other Firebase services, and
it leverages industry standards like OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect, so it can be
easily integrated with your custom backend.
[...]
When you upgrade to Firebase Authentication with Identity Platform, you unlock additional
features, such as multi-factor authentication, blocking functions, user activity
and audit logging, SAML and generic OpenID Connect support, multi
[...]
tenancy,
[...]
identity providers. The Firebase
[...]
methods that allow
[...]
| Custom auth system integration | Connect your app's existing sign-in system to the Firebase Authentication SDK and gain access to Firebase Realtime Database and other Firebase services. iOS Android Web C++ Unity |
[...]
This upgrade does not require any migration---your
existing client SDK and admin SDK code will continue to work as before, and
you'll gain immediate access to features such as enhanced logging and
enterprise-grade support and SLAs. With some additional code, you'll be able to
add multi-factor auth, blocking functions, and support for SAML and OpenID
Connect providers.
[...]
| Enterprise support and SLA | Upgraded projects get
[...]
commitments for Auth services according to the Identity Platform Service Level Agreement (SLA) and are eligible to upgrade to enterprise-grade support. |
[...]
> [!NOTE]
> Note: By default, authenticated users can read and write data to the Firebase Realtime Database and Cloud Storage. You can control the access of those users by modifying your Firebase Realtime Database and Cloud Storage Security Rules.
